name: gamma_satellite_architecture
description: Architectural archetype: human gamma-satellite. ~220 bp GC-rich tandem repeat units found in pericentromeric regions of chromosomes 8 (especially), X, Y, and acrocentric short arms. Smaller arrays than alpha-satellite; identified in T2T-CHM13 with fully resolved arrays.

architecture.internal_structure: ~220 bp GC-rich (~55-60% GC) tandem unit; arrays of 10s to 100s of kb in pericentric regions; most prominent on chr8, chrX, chrY; smaller arrays on acrocentric short arms.
